message:
feat(abi): port 16 nis_* prints + group ops + nis_destroy_object (bd-qk9v5)

Continue NIS+ libnsl parity. Sixteen more nis_* fail-safe entries.

Print helpers (9) — every one writes a constant "(NIS+ unsupported)\n"
line to stdout via libc::write(1, ...). Argument values are accepted
but never dereferenced, so NULL/garbage pointers are safe:
  - nis_print_directory / nis_print_entry / nis_print_group /
    nis_print_group_entry / nis_print_link / nis_print_object /
    nis_print_result / nis_print_rights / nis_print_table

Group ops (6) — return NIS+ error codes:
  - nis_creategroup / nis_destroygroup / nis_addmember /
    nis_removemember / nis_verifygroup all return
    NIS_NAMEUNREACHABLE (= 5).
  - nis_ismember returns 0 (FALSE — bool_t is just int) since no
    membership claim can be verified without a real NIS+ runtime.

Cleanup (1):
  - nis_destroy_object(*obj) returns 0 (NIS_SUCCESS) and is a true
    no-op because our query layer never allocates real NIS+ objects.

Tests: 7 abi tests covering the print-no-crash invariant for both
NULL and arbitrary-pointer inputs across all nine print helpers,
NIS_NAMEUNREACHABLE return for each group-mutation op, ismember
returning 0 in all input combinations (including NULL pointers), and
nis_destroy_object accepting NULL + bogus pointers safely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
